name: stage2-rvv-low-precision-contraction-surface
title: Stage2 RVV low-precision contraction primitive-surface campaign
description: Macro campaign for Stage2 RVV low-precision contraction primitive-surface maturity. Latest completed slice canonicalized the packed-i4 Gearbox stable resource-schedule contract so schedule/resource-cost facts are shared by Gearbox candidate selection, provider validation, and target validation while remediation, admission, no-win, and same-target evidence remain policy/evidence facts.
creator: codex
notes: Foundation outcome completed: the low-precision primitive typed surface and provider validation cover signed i8 and unsigned u8 product-reduction facts. Selected-body realization outcome completed for bounded signed and unsigned representatives: tcrv_rvv.typed_widening_product_reduce_pre_realized_body carries source_signedness, verifier/provider validation accepts only signedness-consistent product-reduction contracts, and RVV selected-body realization materializes explicit setvl/with_vl/load/widening_product/standalone_reduce/store structure before route construction. Provider/artifact carry-through outcome completed for the plain signed/unsigned product-reduction representative slice: route descriptions carry a provider-owned low-precision primitive route payload, emission metadata serializes tcrv_rvv.low_precision_primitive.* only from that payload, and target artifact validation compares candidate primitive mirrors against the provider payload before artifact acceptance. Recent packed-i4 policy-boundary cleanup separated load/unpack, realization, schedule, resource-cost, runtime ABI, and target facts from realization-admission, remediation planning, performance feedback, admission, maturity, dispatch preference, and same-target evidence. Latest slice completed Gearbox stable resource-schedule canonicalization: RVVLowPrecisionPackedI4StableResourceScheduleFacts now owns the accepted packed-i4 schedule-decision, unroll/accumulator/region/live-vector budget, and resource-cost facts consumed by Gearbox candidate selection, handoff schedule verification, provider route planning, and target artifact validation. The accepted stable schedule decision no longer depends on remediation plan, performance admission, beyond-local admission, dispatch preference, no-win classification, same-target evidence IDs, or campaign outcome strings. Macro task remains open for adjacent low-precision primitive-surface cleanup and future fresh source-backed same-target measurement-disposition work if later claimed.
